methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $39.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $3.2B - $14.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Skild AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series C), post-money $14B+

data.narrative:
Figure AI, a Series C AI & Software company founded by Brett Adcock, has raised over $1B and carries a current post-money valuation of $39.0B. The valuation assessment draws on a comp universe of 9 private peers (including Skild AI at $14.0B, Wayve at $8.6B, and Physical Intelligence at $5.6B) and 1 public comparable (Palantir), selected for stage and sector alignment.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement, implies a forward valuation of $66.3B from the $39.0B base. Peer-median analysis across 4 same-sector and same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $7.1B, with a range spanning $3.2B to $14.0B. The divergence between these methods reflects inherent tension between Figure AI's current size relative to peers and typical progression mechanics.

The triangulated valuation range is $6.0B (low) to $76.2B (high), with a median of $36.7B. The current $39.0B valuation aligns closely with the median estimate. Key uncertainties include the applicability of stage-progression multipliers given Figure AI's already-elevated Series C valuation relative to peer medians, and the degree to which frontier AI valuations reflect executable business models versus technology optionality. The wide range underscores these fundamental assessment challenges.
